title: 9 Herbal Tinctures For Dermatitis
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures can be a great way to soothe and calm skin affected by dermatitis. When we drink herbal teas, the active ingredients in the herbs are absorbed into our bloodstream and can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the skin. For example, Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to calm red, itchy skin. Aloe barbadensis, or aloe vera, is another herb that's great for soothing skin irritations, as it has cooling and moisturizing properties that can help to calm and hydrate the skin. Urtica dioica, or stinging nettle, has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that can help to reduce itching and inflammation in the skin. These herbs can be made into teas, or taken in tincture form, and can be a great addition to a daily skincare routine. They can help to reduce the frequency and severity of dermatitis flare-ups, and can even help to promote healthy, glowing skin. By incorporating these herbs into your life, you may find that you have fewer skin irritations and more confidence in your own skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tinctures contains bioactive constituents like triterpenoids, flavonoids, and carotenoids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with dermatitis. The tincture also contains saponins, which have antiseptic properties that can help prevent infection and promote healing in the affected skin. Additionally, calendula's high antioxidant content can protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting a healthy skin environment. By applying calendula officinalis tinctures to the skin, the bioactive constituents can work together to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the symptoms of dermatitis.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal tinctures for dermatitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Calendula flowers and a clean glass jar.
Combine 1 cup of dried Calendula flowers with 2 cups of vodka in the glass jar. Close the jar.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Store the tincture in the fridge and use 5-10 drops, 2-3 times a day, to soothe dermatitis symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tinctures contains aloe-emodin, aloin, and acemannan. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help calm skin irritations associated with dermatitis. Aloe-emodin and aloin have been shown to reduce redness and swelling, while acemannan promotes wound healing and tissue repair. The anti-inflammatory properties of these compounds can also help reduce skin itching and discomfort. The combination of these bioactive constituents in aloe barbadensis tinctures can help provide relief from dermatitis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal tinctures for dermatitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Aloe barbadensis leaves and 1 cup of 80% vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar.
Wash the Aloe leaves with water and dry them with a clean cloth.
Add the dried Aloe leaves to the glass jar with the vodka or glycerin. Make sure the leaves are completely covered.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day. This will help the Aloe release its active ingredients into the vodka or glycerin.
After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the Aloe leaves and store the tincture in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica tinctures contains bioactive constituents such as histamine, quercetin, and polysaccharides, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help to reduce redness and swelling associated with dermatitis. Quercetin, a flavonoid present in the tincture, has been shown to inhibit the production of inflammatory mediators that contribute to skin inflammation. The polysaccharides in Urtica dioica tincture may also help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing itching and discomfort. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Urtica dioica tinctures can help to alleviate the symptoms of dermatitis.
remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal tinctures for dermatitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Urtica dioica leaves, stems, and flowers.
Combine the Urtica dioica plant material with 2 cups of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Discard the solids and store the Urtica dioica tincture in a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid. Use 20-30 drops, 2-3 times a day, to help with dermatitis symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum tinctures contains hyperforin and hypericin, two key bioactive constituents that help reduce inflammation and itching associated with dermatitis. These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and swelling. Hyperforin also has antihistamine-like effects, which can help alleviate allergic reactions and skin irritation. Additionally, hypericum perforatum tinctures contain flavonoids, such as quercetin, which have antioxidant properties that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. By using hypericum perforatum tinctures, individuals with dermatitis may experience relief from symptoms such as itching, redness, and inflammation.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal tinctures for dermatitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Hypericum perforatum flowers and 2 cups of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Fill the jar with the fresh flowers, leaving about 1 inch of space at the top.
Pour the vodka over the flowers, making sure they are completely covered.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Store the tincture in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tinctures contains alkylamides, glycosides, and phenolic acids. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The alkylamides, specifically cis- and trans-11-alkylamides, help to reduce inflammation and soothe the skin. The glycosides, particularly echinacoside, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals. By reducing inflammation and promoting skin health, Echinacea purpurea tinctures may be beneficial for people suffering from dermatitis.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal tinctures for dermatitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and 2 cups of vodka (at least 80 proof).
Combine the dried flowers and vodka in a clean glass jar. Stir well to make sure the flowers are covered.
Place the jar in a dark place and let it sit for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day. This is called 'steeping'.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid. Use 20-30 drops, 2-3 times a day, for skin issues like dermatitis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hamamelis virginiana tinctures contains salicylic acid, a key bioactive constituent that helps reduce inflammation and soothe skin irritations associated with dermatitis. The anti-inflammatory properties of salicylic acid make it effective in calming red, itchy skin and promoting healing. Hamamelis virginiana also contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. The astringent properties of hamamelis help reduce swelling and ease skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis by tightening the skin and reducing the amount of moisture that can exacerbate the condition. By using hamamelis virginiana tinctures, individuals with dermatitis can experience relief from symptoms and promote healthy skin regeneration.
remedies.image_alt: Hamamelis virginiana herbal tinctures for dermatitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Hamamelis virginiana leaves and stems. Clean them with water.
Use a blender or food processor to chop the leaves and stems into small pieces.
Transfer the chopped plant material to a clean glass jar and add 4 cups of 80-proof vodka. Close the jar.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day. This allows the plant to infuse into the vodka.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the plant material. Use the tincture as directed for dermatitis relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale tinctures contains bioactive constituents like allantoin, aucubin, and rosmarinic acid, which give it anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. These compounds help reduce redness and swelling associated with dermatitis. Allantoin, in particular, is known for its ability to promote wound healing and tissue repair, making it an effective treatment for skin irritations. The tannins and flavonoids in Symphytum officinale tinctures also have astringent properties, which can help to reduce itching and inflammation. By reducing inflammation and promoting tissue repair, Symphytum officinale tinctures can be a helpful natural remedy for dermatitis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Symphytum officinale herbal tinctures for dermatitis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of fresh Symphytum officinale leaves, 1 cup of 80% vodka, and a clean glass jar.
Combine the fresh Symphytum officinale leaves and vodka in the glass jar, making sure the leaves are fully covered by the vodka.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day to mix the ingredients.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Label the jar and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 20-30 drops of the tincture, 2-3 times a day, to help relieve dermatitis symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale tinctures contains a combination of bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The flavonoids in Taraxacum officinale have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with dermatitis. The phenolic acids in the tincture have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. The sesquiterpenes in Taraxacum officinale have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe and heal the skin. By addressing the underlying causes of dermatitis, Taraxacum officinale tinctures can help alleviate symptoms and promote skin health.
remedies.image_alt: Taraxacum officinale herbal tinctures for dermatitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers. Clean them with water.
Add 2 cups of 80% ethanol to a clean glass jar. Pour the Taraxacum officinale mixture on top.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks. Shake the jar every day.
After 2 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid. Use 20-30 drops, 3 times a day, as needed to treat dermatitis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Saponaria officinalis tinctures contains saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are beneficial for dermatitis. The saponins in Saponaria officinalis tinctures can help reduce skin inflammation and promote wound healing. The flavonoids, particularly quercetin, have antihistamine properties that can soothe skin irritation and itching associated with dermatitis. The phenolic acids, such as caffeic acid, have antimicrobial properties that can help prevent infection and promote skin health.
remedies.image_alt: Saponaria officinalis herbal tinctures for dermatitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Saponaria officinalis leaves and flowers. Clean and dry them.
Fill a clean glass jar with 2 cups of vodka or glycerin. You can use either one.
Add the dried Saponaria officinalis to the jar, making sure they are completely covered by the liquid.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks. Shake the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container. Discard the solids. Your Saponaria officinalis tincture is now ready to use.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help soothe skin irritations like dermatitis. Some herbal teas, such as those made from calendula or chamomile, have anti-inflammatory properties that can calm the skin. These teas may reduce redness and swelling, making them a potential natural remedy for dermatitis.
faq_safe: While herbal teas may help soothe dermatitis symptoms, drinking them every day can be safe or not, depending on the tea and your body. Some teas like chamomile or calendula are generally considered gentle and may be a good choice. However, stronger or more potent teas like dandelion or burdock root can cause issues if consumed excessively.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas for dermatitis relief might start working within a few days to a week. Some people notice improvement in their skin symptoms, like reduced redness and itching, within 2-3 days. However, it may take up to 2 weeks to see significant results, as herbal teas work slowly to soothe and calm the skin.
faq_time: For skin issues like dermatitis, drinking herbal tea in the morning can be helpful. This is because morning tea can help reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin throughout the day. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, also have calming effects that can ease skin problems.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for dermatitis, but it depends on the type of tea and medicine. Some herbal teas, like chamomile and passionflower, have compounds that can slow down liver functions, which can affect how well the medicine works. This is a concern for people taking certain medications to treat dermatitis.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant and dealing with dermatitis, you might consider herbal teas for relief. However, some herbal teas can be too strong or even trigger allergic reactions. For example, chamomile and peppermint teas are generally considered safe, but teas like pennyroyal and blue cohosh have potential risks, especially when consumed in large amounts.
faq_breastfeeding: If you have dermatitis while breastfeeding, you can try drinking herbal teas to help soothe your skin. However, some herbs can pass into your milk, so it's essential to choose ones that are known to be safe for breastfeeding. Look for teas made from ingredients like calendula, chamomile, or peppermint, which are often used for skin issues.
